description: openclaw agent --agent main with memory-only prompts returns no reliable hits for high-level user-profile questions even after Slack history ingest and successful indexing (files/chunks populated). Investigate retrieval path mismatch between agent/main and CLI memory search, improve query grounding over indexed slack-history corpus, and add verification checks proving memory-only Q/A works for 3 difficult prompts.
notes: Confirmed root-cause path mismatch: gateway memory tool runs without OPENAI_API_KEY in launchd env. Evidence: (a) session tool results show provider:none/mode:fts-only; (b) gateway logs repeatedly show 'Skipping memory file sync in FTS-only mode (no embedding provider)'; (c) live gateway processes report OPENAI_API_KEY=UNSET; (d) launchd plist at ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist lacks OPENAI_API_KEY; (e) auth resolver code for provider=openai throws when only openai-codex OAuth exists and OPENAI_API_KEY is absent, causing provider null for memory embeddings. CLI shell commands can still show provider=openai when OPENAI_API_KEY is set in interactive shell, creating the observed mismatch.
title: Fix memory-only retrieval/synthesis gap in openclaw agent main

Produce a focused PR with docs/config/policy updates and minimal code changes needed to enforce behavior. In first update, report exact file list to be changed before implementation.
title: OpenClaw autopilot policy PR

description:
Scope: immediate triage plan from rev-ljq investigation.

Proposed actions:
1) Fail closed on provider exhaustion
- Ensure primary/fallback model set does not include profiles in long cooldown loops during incidents.
- Add explicit health check gate before dispatching new high-cost runs when provider auth profile is unavailable/rate-limited.

2) Concurrency pressure control (config-first)
- Candidate one-line change for fast mitigation: reduce agents.defaults.maxConcurrent from 4 -> 2 until error rate normalizes.
- Re-evaluate agents.defaults.subagents.maxConcurrent (currently 8); cap lower during incident mode.

3) OOM prevention
- Add runtime memory watchdog/alert and restart policy guard when heap approaches limit.
- Capture heap stats with lane wait diagnostics in same event bundle.

4) Alert thresholds
- Critical alert: lane wait exceeded with waitedMs >= 60000 for main lane, or queueAhead >= 20 sustained for 3+ minutes.
- Major alert: repeated FailoverError rate-limit/auth-profile events > N/min with concurrent lane waits.

5) Noise reduction
- Resolve tools.allow/tools.profile unknown-entry warnings and missing MCP dependencies so diagnostics surface true blockers.

Acceptance criteria:
- Config PR or explicit config diff documented for concurrency mitigation.
- Alert thresholds codified (script/config) and verified against historical log sample.
- Post-change run shows no new waitedMs > 60s in comparable load test window.
title: Remediation: openclaw lane saturation + provider failure guardrails

description:
Dockerfile.selftest launches cmux-gtk with GTK_A11Y=none and DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS=disabled: killing AT-SPI and causing 15 test failures (8 in layer3_antifake, 7 in layer1_antifake).

Create scripts/run-layer1antifake.sh and scripts/run-layer3antifake.sh using same restart pattern as run-layer1.sh:
1. Kill existing cmux-gtk
2. Start fresh dbus-daemon, save address to /tmp/dbus_addr.txt
3. Relaunch app WITHOUT GTK_A11Y=none, WITH real DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS
4. Wait 5s for AT-SPI registration  
5. Run tests/layer1_antifake.py or layer3_antifake.py

See ~/Downloads/linux-gtk-tests-100pct-plan.md Fix 1.
title: Add AT-SPI-enabled run scripts for antifake test suites

description:
B1-B5 and D2-D3 tests (7 failures) type shell commands via xdotool into the VTE terminal but the PTY never receives the input, so output files are never written.

Root cause: xdotool types into the focused X window but the VTE widget may not have focus when tests run.

Fix in tests/layer1_antifake.py: Replace shell_exec_to_file() to use cmux socket's SendInput command instead of xdotool. Socket injection is more reliable (no X focus dependency) and tests the real PTY path.

Steps:
1. Discover exact socket API: run python3 against /tmp/cmux-core.sock with method='Help' or 'ListCommands'
2. Implement socket_send_to_surface(uuid, text) using the socket API
3. Update shell_exec_to_file() to get active surface UUID then inject via socket
4. If socket API lacks SendInput, fallback: add xdotool windowfocus before typing

See ~/Downloads/linux-gtk-tests-100pct-plan.md Fix 2.
title: Replace xdotool terminal input with socket injection in antifake tests

description:
Before implementing socket-based terminal injection in the antifake tests, need to confirm the exact method name and protocol for sending input to a VTE surface.

Run inside Docker container:
  python3 -c "
import socket, json
s = socket.socket(socket.AF_UNIX)
s.connect('/tmp/cmux-core.sock')
s.send(json.dumps({'method': 'Help', 'params': {}}).encode() + b'\n')
print(s.recv(65536).decode()[:3000])
"

Look for methods like: SendInput, SendText, TypeText, InjectInput, or similar.
Also check if API is GraphQL, line-delimited JSON, or plain text.

Document: method name, parameter names, how to specify target surface UUID.
title: Discover cmux socket API for terminal input injection

description:
Updated investigation (2026-03-10/11):

Key corrections and findings:
- Thread context p1773207801 maps to 2026-03-10 22:43:21 PDT (epoch).
- In that exact window (~22:42-22:45 local), only one lane-wait event was observed: `lane=session:agent:main:main waitedMs=3323 queueAhead=0` at 22:42:53.
- The previously cited 28k-78k ms wait cluster (02:48-02:53) is real, but it is a separate time window from p1773207801.

Primary saturation episode (separate severe incident):
- Very large main-lane backlog observed around 20:57-20:59 local with queueAhead ~222-227 and waitedMs up to 1,163,431 ms (~19.4m).
- Same window shows repeated provider failures and lane task errors (FailoverError rate-limit/auth-profile related) and agent runtime distress.
- Node process then hit heap OOM (`FATAL ERROR: Reached heap limit Allocation failed - JavaScript heap out of memory`).

Interpretation:
- Evidence supports OpenClaw lane saturation + downstream provider/tool instability as root contributors during heavy incidents.
- For p1773207801 specifically, available logs do not show an equivalent severe lane backlog at the exact thread timestamp; delay may include model/tool execution latency and noisy plugin/tool config issues rather than pure queue depth.
- mctrl `.messages` / `.tracking` files remain orchestration bookkeeping, not OpenClaw lane queue state.
title: Investigate high OpenClaw lane backlog from Slack thread p1773207801

description: 50Q eval via openclaw agent is unstable due two runtime blockers: (1) recurring lock contention on main session file b643bf09...jsonl.lock causing immediate model failures, and (2) model path timeouts/rate-limit fallback causing NO_ID outcomes unrelated to memory quality. Need durable fix: isolate eval agent/session, clear stale lock handling, and enforce provider availability checks before run. Add deterministic preflight in scripts/mem0_50q_run.sh and fail-fast diagnostics in output.
title: Stabilize OpenClaw agent memory eval path (session lock + timeout)
